pattern: A power of four is a positive number with a single set bit that lands on an even bit position, which two bit masks can verify without any loop.
hints: A power of four is first of all a power of two, so it must have exactly one set bit.
Among powers of two, only those whose single bit sits at an even position (1, 4, 16, 64, ...) are powers of four.
Check n > 0, that n & (n - 1) == 0 for a single bit, and that the bit lies in the mask 0x55555555 covering even positions.

edge_inputs.description: sixteen is a power of four
five is neither a power of two nor four
one is four to the zeroth power
two is a power of two but not of four
eight is a power of two at an odd bit position
zero is not a power of four
negative number is never a power of four
largest power of four within the int range

stress.description: wide range of ints, mostly non-powers with occasional true powers of four
stress.note: single integer; both branches respect the int32 bound
